source.title: Beta Oscillations in Working Memory, Executive Control of Movement and Thought, and Sensorimotor Function

confidence_rationale: Direct support for beta as a 'maintain current state' / status-quo signal in basal ganglia-cortical circuits; evidence that beta gates propagation to action selection rather than internal model updating; the hyperdirect pathway as fast interrupt mechanism is well evidenced. Main caveat: most direct evidence is from pathological states (Parkinson's) or motor cortex; PFC/E3-equivalent is more inferential.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Beta band oscillations (~13-30 Hz) in the basal ganglia and prefrontal cortex signal a 'maintain current state / status quo' that opposes both movement initiation and cognitive updating; elevated prefrontal beta during working memory delay prevents distractor interference; the hyperdirect cortex-STN pathway propagates high-beta to enforce rapid braking.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-090: Beta elevated during committed action sequence → E3 policy-output to action selection is gated/held. E3 continues internal model updating (heartbeat continues) but update does not propagate. At sequence completion or stop-change: beta drops → E3 state propagates to action selector. Hyperdirect pathway (cortex → STN) implements the fast interrupt for mid-sequence emergency stop.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Most direct STN beta evidence is from Parkinson's disease populations where beta is pathologically amplified; whether the same gating principle operates in healthy agentic action sequences requires caution. PFC delay-period beta is more directly relevant to E3 but the propagation-gating distinction (internal vs output) is not always explicitly tested.
mapping.source_context: Review of beta oscillations in human EEG/LFP, PD patients, and primate recordings; motor, working memory, and cognitive control paradigms.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality; the status-quo framing is well-established; transfer risk is moderate given PD population bias in STN data.
